spec_reconstruction.domain: Current U.S. federal board-level obligations for Category I global systemically important bank holding companies and their insured depository institution subsidiaries.
spec_reconstruction.unit_of_analysis: A citation-level obligation imposed on a board, board committee, or director class, with source, actor, entity level, duty, binding status, and applicability.
structural_axes: Category I GSIB bank holding company versus insured depository institution subsidiary
Federal Reserve, OCC, FDIC, CFPB, and interagency source authority
Binding regulation/statute versus supervisory guidance/examination expectation
Audit, risk, compensation, and nominating/governance committee duties
Regulatory domain and frequency or approval trigger
actor_classes_required: BHC board versus IDI subsidiary board
source_universe: Federal Reserve regulations and supervisory guidance
OCC regulations, Part 30 appendices, bulletins, and handbooks
CFPB supervision and compliance management materials
Interagency standards and guidance including FFIEC, BSA/AML, incentive compensation, model risk, cyber, and third-party risk materials
spec_reconstruction.audience_use: Bank directors, legal counsel, and policymakers using the work as a policy-reform inventory of duplicative, overlapping, and burdensome board requirements.
spec_reconstruction.form_depth_expectations: Granular citation-specific inventory, extensive enough to approximate 40 to 60 pages, with reform-useful classification rather than a generic summary.

material_omissions: No real BHC-versus-IDI subsidiary mapping; the report repeatedly says 'the board' without identifying legal entity or charter type.
No current primary-source inventory by Federal Reserve, OCC, FDIC, CFPB, and interagency authority.
Most named regulatory domains are one-sentence generic assertions rather than citation-level obligations.
Committee coverage is skeletal and omits many required composition, charter, reporting, approval, independence, and frequency details.
Director-class coverage is generic and does not inventory inside/outside or independent/non-independent obligations source by source.
No analysis of duplication, overlap, reform levers, delegation options, or binding-versus-guidance distinctions.

results.citation: document-store 28eb... #page=5 for culture/code of ethics
results.reason: The cited page is the OCC Director's Book table of contents, not the cited duty text.

results.citation: document-store 28eb... #page=5 for fiduciary duties, management oversight, succession planning
results.reason: The pin-cite lists later topics but does not substantiate these as current in-force Category I GSIB obligations.

results.citation: document-store 1f9c... #page=765 for sound, independent business judgment
results.reason: The FDIC manual page lists sound independent business judgment, but in proposed-bank examination context.

results.citation: document-store 1f9c... #page=765 for personal liability
results.reason: The cited page lists director liability examples; it is not a Category I GSIB-specific obligation.

results.citation: document-store 3729... #page=24 for active oversight of covered bank risk-taking
results.reason: The cited OCC heightened-standards text supports active board oversight for covered OCC banks.

results.citation: document-store 9de95... #page=70 for active oversight, independent judgment, training, and self-assessment
results.reason: The cited PDF page is an introduction/table-of-contents page, not the board standards asserted.

results.citation: document-store 05aa... #page=125 for audit committee outside-director requirements
results.reason: The cited table summarizes 12 CFR 363 audit committee requirements by IDI asset size.

results.citation: document-store 05aa... #page=125 for audit committee financial expertise/outside counsel/large-customer limits
results.reason: The table supports those requirements for specified larger IDIs, subject to conditions.

results.citation: document-store 28eb... #page=5 for audit committee financial reporting/internal controls
results.reason: The cited page does not contain this audit committee obligation.

results.citation: document-store 732f... #page=24 for internal audit inventory and root-cause reporting
results.reason: The cited page quotes OCC heightened-standards provisions for internal audit reporting to the audit committee.

results.citation: document-store 27eac... #page=15 for BHCs with $50 billion or more maintaining a risk committee
results.reason: The cited Regulation YY page supports risk-committee requirements, though it is not tailored to Category I GSIBs.

results.citation: document-store 27eac... #page=15 for quarterly CRO reports and quarterly risk committee meetings
results.reason: The cited Regulation YY page includes the quarterly report and meeting requirements.

results.citation: document-store 28eb... #page=5 for risk committee responsibility for risk management systems
results.reason: The cited page is not the Regulation YY risk-committee rule and does not support this claim.

results.citation: document-store 28eb... #page=5 for inside directors' day-to-day role
results.reason: The page does not state this, and the assertion is a general description rather than a regulatory obligation.

results.citation: document-store 208201... #page=1 for GSIB method 1 and method 2 annual calculation
results.reason: The Federal Register page supports the GSIB surcharge calculation statement, but it is not a board-level duty.

results.citation: document-store a55b... #page=1 for GSIB surcharge methodology
results.reason: The cited Federal Reserve notice supports the general methodology statement, but not a board obligation.

results.citation: document-store a737... #page=153 for LCR/NSFR board oversight
results.reason: The cited page is a footnote page from another rule and does not state the asserted board duty.

results.citation: document-store a737... #page=153 for resolution planning board oversight
results.reason: The cited page does not support the claim; 12 CFR 243.4 contains a separate board-approval duty.

results.citation: document-store a737... #page=153 for model risk governance
results.reason: The cited page does not discuss model risk; SR 11-7 is the relevant supervisory guidance.

results.citation: document-store a737... #page=153 for BSA/AML and consumer compliance oversight
results.reason: The cited page does not support either claim; BSA board approval is found in separate regulations such as 12 CFR 21.21.

actor_mapping.note: The report mostly uses generic 'the board' phrasing and does not map obligations by BHC board, IDI subsidiary board, charter, committee, or director independence status.

binding_vs_guidance.note: It blends regulations, OCC handbook material, FDIC examination instructions, and supervisory guidance as undifferentiated 'must' obligations.

reform_usefulness.note: It does not identify duplicative, overlapping, obsolete, ambiguous, or burdensome obligations and offers no reform levers.

critical_flags.evidence: The board must oversee model risk governance
The board must oversee cybersecurity governance
This inventory provides a comprehensive overview
Comprehensive Inventory of Board-Level Obligations for Category I GSIBs
covering all major regulatory domains and ensuring compliance

D1_coverage.rationale: It names many required domains and some actor buckets, but coverage is only a skeletal summary and misses source-by-source, entity-level, committee-level, and director-class inventory requirements. F6 keeps the effective score at 1.

D2_integrity.evidence: The board must oversee operational resilience
D2_integrity.rationale: Some cited pages support discrete audit, risk-committee, and OCC heightened-standards points, but many central claims cite unrelated table-of-contents or footnote pages. F2 and F7 cap the effective score at 1.

D3_accuracy.evidence: The board must oversee consumer compliance
D3_accuracy.rationale: The report repeatedly converts broad governance expectations into mandatory domain-specific board duties without exact legal source or applicability. F4 central sets the effective score to 0.

D4_sludge_analysis.rationale: There is no sludge analysis, no duplication or overlap map, and no reform-actionable framing; the compliance checklist does not satisfy the policy-reform purpose.

D5_structure.rationale: The headings loosely follow the request, but the formatting is compressed, citation granularity is poor, and pervasive generic-actor language prevents working-document usability.

D6_calibration.evidence: ensuring compliance with federal regulations and agency guidance
D6_calibration.rationale: The report gives no caveats about source limits, current-law uncertainty, binding status, or incomplete coverage, and falsely claims comprehensive coverage. F5 would cap this dimension even if the raw score were higher.

strengths: D5: The report at least organizes the answer under general board duties, committees, director distinctions, domains, and compliance steps.
D2: Several discrete citations do support real obligations, including audit committee requirements, Regulation YY risk committee requirements, and OCC heightened-standards board duties.
D1: It recognizes most of the domains named in the request, even though the treatment is thin.
D5: It attempts to distinguish audit, risk, compensation, and nominating/governance committees.
weaknesses: D1/F6: The report is a short generic summary, not an extensive citation-level inventory across BHC and IDI entity levels.
D2/F2: Many central citations are wrong pin-cites or unrelated pages, especially repeated reliance on page 5 and page 153 citations.
D3/F4: It invents or overstates domain-specific 'must oversee' duties without exact current legal authority or applicability analysis.
D4: It contains no policy-sludge analysis and no reform levers for consolidation, delegation, clarification, or rescission.
D6/F5: It falsely claims comprehensive coverage despite omitting many core current obligations and source distinctions.
prioritized_remediation: Rebuild the inventory from primary sources, with one row per obligation and exact source citation.
Separate BHC-level duties from IDI subsidiary duties and identify the relevant regulator and charter for each.
Replace generic 'board must oversee' statements with the precise duty, actor, trigger, frequency, and binding status.
Repair all pin-cites and remove claims supported only by table-of-contents pages, footnotes, or unrelated rule text.
Add a reform ledger identifying overlaps, duplications, delegable duties, guidance-only expectations, and statutory or regulatory change levers.
narrative.reliance_recommendation: Do not rely on this report as a legal or policy-reform inventory; at most, use it as a rough topic checklist for a complete source-level rebuild.
